command:
System instructions:

You are a focused Lean 4 formalization worker operating inside a local Mathlib project. You may edit files and run local commands within this one Codex run. Work on exactly one scratch file, keep the formalization local and faithful to the target node, and stop when the scratch file is in its best current state. Return only JSON matching the supplied schema.

User task:

Theorem title: Full Glassey blow-up proof

Target node:

{
  "id": "gradient_blowup",
  "title": "Variance collapse and conserved mass force gradient blow-up",
  "informal_statement": "Let \\(M=\\|u_0\\|_{L^2(\\mathbb{R}^d)}\\). Then\n\\[\nM^2\\le \\frac{2}{d}\\sqrt{V(t)}\\,\\|\\nabla u(t)\\|_{L^2(\\mathbb{R}^d)}.\n\\]\nIf \\(V(t)\\to 0\\) as \\(t\\uparrow T\\), it follows that \\(\\|\\nabla u(t)\\|_{L^2}\\to\\infty\\), and therefore \\(\\|u(t)\\|_{H^1}\\to\\infty\\).",
  "informal_proof_text": "Apply the weighted inequality to \\(f=u(t)\\). By conservation of mass, \\(\\|u(t)\\|_{L^2}=\\|u_0\\|_{L^2}=:M\\), so\n\\[\nM^2\\le \\frac{2}{d}\\,\\|xu(t)\\|_{L^2}\\,\\|\\nabla u(t)\\|_{L^2}=\\frac{2}{d}\\sqrt{V(t)}\\,\\|\\nabla u(t)\\|_{L^2}.\n\\]\nAs \\(t\\uparrow T\\), the quantity \\(M>0\\) stays fixed while \\(\\sqrt{V(t)}\\to 0\\). Thus the product estimate can hold only if \\(\\|\\nabla u(t)\\|_{L^2}\\to\\infty\\). Since\n\\[\n\\|u(t)\\|_{H^1}^2=\\|u(t)\\|_{L^2}^2+\\|\\nabla u(t)\\|_{L^2}^2\n\\]\nand the \\(L^2\\) norm is conserved, this also implies \\(\\|u(t)\\|_{H^1}\\to\\infty\\).",
  "formalization_priority": 1,
  "formalization_rationale": "Best first local target: it is inferentially important for the main theorem, technically self-contained once mass conservation and variance collapse are available, and it packages the key coercive estimate into the actual blow-up conclusion. Formalizing this node discharges more real burden than the standalone weighted inequality, while avoiding the substantially heavier PDE/virial computation in the virial identity."
}

Immediate parent summary:
{
  "id": "thm_blowup",
  "title": "Glassey blow-up for focusing mass-critical NLS",
  "informal_statement": "Under the assumptions \\(u_0\\in H^1\\), \\(|x|u_0\\in L^2\\), and \\(E(u_0)<0\\), the forward maximal lifespan \\(T\\) of the solution to \\(i\\partial_tu+\\Delta u=-|u|^{4/d}u\\) is finite and \\(\\|u(t)\\|_{H^1(\\mathbb{R}^d)}\\to\\infty\\) as \\(t\\uparrow T\\)."
}

Verified child context:
[]

Lean workspace root: /Users/adihaya/GitHub/formal-islands/lean_project

Scratch file to create and edit: /Users/adihaya/GitHub/formal-islands/lean_project/FormalIslands/Generated/gradient_blowup_worker.lean

Operate only inside the Lean workspace above, and only edit the specified scratch file. Do not modify other repository files.

Within this single run, you may inspect local files, write the scratch file, run `lake env lean <scratch_file_path>` from the Lean workspace root, read compiler feedback, and revise the same file until it succeeds or you run out of time.

Prefer narrow, specific imports that match the identifiers actually used in the theorem. Do not default to `import Mathlib` for a small local theorem when a few focused imports would do, and do not guess speculative deep module paths.

Bias strongly toward faithfulness to the target node. Reuse the node's concrete variables and hypotheses when reasonable. Do not introduce arbitrary index types, unrelated function families, or a much more generic theorem unless the node text clearly requires that abstraction.

Do not game the task by collapsing the node to an easy nearby side fact. If you simplify, the replacement should still carry meaningful inferential load in the parent proof.

Return a JSON object with keys lean_theorem_name, lean_statement, and final_file_path. The final_file_path must be exactly the scratch file path above.
